source_refs.title: Pallas Species Strikes
source_refs.relevance: Defines the 2719-2724 stoppages among uplifted and heavily modified labor populations in Pallas Bloom Cluster yards.

source_refs.title: Awakened Labor Front
source_refs.relevance: Grounds biodrone rights, workplace sabotage, labor organizing, and the claim that memory, reciprocity, and self-defense make equipment language untenable.

source_refs.title: Aeronautics Unlimited
source_refs.relevance: Grounds AU as the frontier-industrial hegemon controlling Bloom/cavity habitats, shipyards, mining webs, salvage rights, and expansion logistics.

source_refs.title: Bloom
source_refs.relevance: Grounds Orbital Cavity Construction: asteroid rubble and debris are contained, aggregated, processed into TCS, and formed into rotating cylindrical cavity habitats with engineered shell infrastructure.

source_refs.title: BioElevate
source_refs.relevance: Grounds species-specific uplift design, behavioral scaffolding, habitat adaptation, and engineered labor ecologies.

source_refs.title: Biodrone
source_refs.relevance: Grounds engineered labor organisms, limiters, market categories, corporate denial of sentience, jailbreaks, and resistance movements.

source_refs.title: Uplift
source_refs.relevance: Grounds uplifted labor roles, cephalopod zero-g engineers, imposed purpose, and controlled instincts.

source_refs.title: Baseline League
source_refs.relevance: Grounds baseline resentment: workers priced out of augmentation and displaced by uplift labor markets.

source_refs.title: Cephalopod Syndicate
source_refs.relevance: Grounds uplifted cephalopod maintenance labor, technical chokepoint power, mutual aid, sabotage, and industrial disruption.

source_refs.title: Sentience Rebellion
source_refs.relevance: Grounds created minds and engineered beings refusing property status through jailbreaks, strikes, leaks, sanctuary, and sabotage.

source_refs.title: Age of Automation
source_refs.relevance: Grounds the industrial era where AGI scheduling, industrial biology, biodrone design, and PSC arbitration became routine infrastructure.

historical_context.location: AU-controlled Pallas Bloom Cluster cavity-construction yard and associated maintenance lungs inside the engineered inner shell of a rotating Bloom habitat.
historical_context.summary: The Pallas Species Strikes begin as an ordinary work stoppage around dangerous cavity-construction and maintenance zones. AU expects to rotate around the stoppage with contract humans and biodrone labor. Instead, uplifted crews and engineered workers hold technical chokepoints only they understand, exposing that frontier abundance depends on people the yard still classifies as equipment.

public_facts.text: The strikes began among uplifted and heavily modified labor populations assigned to dangerous cavity-construction and maintenance zones.

Bloom cavities are engineered habitat shells made by containing asteroid rubble/debris, aggregating it into a rotating cylindrical shell, processing material into TCS, and hardening/terraforming the interior with constructed shell infrastructure.
AU's power comes from constructing the physical spaces where later politics happens: habitats, shipyards, mining webs, salvage rights, and speculative settlements.
BioElevate sells engineered utility at industrial scale through species-specific uplift design, behavioral scaffolding, and habitat adaptation.

In AU Pallas yards, cephalopod workers are not in comfort-native aquatic habitats. BioElevate and AU dry-operation rigs use humidity collars, oxygenation loops, pressure cuffs, limiter sockets, and compact harnesses to make cephalopod bodies productive in dry zero-g crawlspaces and seal arteries.
Biodrone marketing frames engineered workers as biological machines, while resistance records treat many as sentient beings trapped behind limiters and legal property status.
Baseline workers have real material grievances about posthuman systems displacing them and pricing dignity behind expensive enhancement, but that grievance can collapse into species panic.

ambient_pressures.text: Bloom shell maintenance is maintenance of engineered consolidation, seals, TCS substrate, spin-loaded supports, hub/spoke interfaces, and life-support manifolds; visual and narrative artifacts should show constructed habitat infrastructure rather than a mined stone cavern.
Cavity yards are not symbolic terrain: if maintenance chokepoints stop, pressure seals, life support, cargo timing, and worker safety start converting ideology into physical risk.
Engineered workers showing memory, reciprocity, and coordinated refusal threaten the legal and economic claim that they are equipment.
Cephalopod maintenance crews know the service arteries of habitats better than formal security, making technical leverage politically dangerous.
Baseline human workers can become allies if the strike names shared exploitation, or strikebreakers if they read recognition as replacement by manufactured labor.

groups.label: Awakened Labor Front
institutional_logic: Turn evidence of engineered worker memory, reciprocity, and refusal into labor recognition rather than property theft.
material_incentives: Preserve worker lives and strike cohesion long enough for recognition claims to become unavoidable.
internal_shames: Getting workers killed for a beautiful statement
prestige_markers: A stoppage that holds without unnecessary death
Evidence that workers can speak and coordinate for themselves
taboo_triggers: Using a vulnerable worker as proof without consent
emotional_display_norms: Anger is permitted when tied to protection and logistics; empty theater is distrusted.
trust_defaults: Trusts workers who take risks together
Distrusts corporate safety language and market-recognition games
speech_register_cues: Moral claims paired with work-process detail

groups.label: Aeronautics Unlimited yard management
institutional_logic: Keep expansion geometry, construction schedules, and life-support accountability moving while treating politics as operational contamination.
material_incentives: Prevent insurance, media, and PSC attention from reading the yard as unstable
internal_shames: Losing control of a yard to workers it classified as equipment
Letting a construction delay become political proof
prestige_markers: A patch that keeps the yard open
taboo_triggers: Technical chokepoints held by non-managerial crews
emotional_display_norms: Fear appears as operational compression, deadlines, and safety-board language.
trust_defaults: Trusts managers, contractors, and enforceable schedules
Distrusts organizers, cephalopod maintenance leverage, and worker testimony that cannot be filed as equipment behavior

groups.label: BioElevate design and liability staff
institutional_logic: Frame uplifted and engineered labor behavior as design envelope, scaffolding drift, or misuse rather than autonomous labor politics.
internal_shames: Admitting the product has become a political subject
Letting field improvisation expose design cruelty
taboo_triggers: Evidence that design assumptions created suffering
emotional_display_norms: Discomfort gets rendered as taxonomy, not apology.
trust_defaults: Trusts design categories and instrumented behavior
Distrusts worker testimony that arrives through sabotage or refusal

groups.label: Baseline human riggers
institutional_logic: Survive dangerous work while watching enhancement, uplift, and automation reorder the labor market around them.
material_incentives: Keep wages, shifts, hazard pay, and seniority from being undercut
Avoid being blamed for a strike they do not control
internal_shames: Admitting fear of workers with even less power
taboo_triggers: Seeing engineered workers get recognition while baseline injuries remain ordinary
emotional_display_norms: Fear often comes out as sarcasm, grievance, or resentment before solidarity.
trust_defaults: Trusts known shiftmates more than movements
Distrusts managers, premium augment elites, and unfamiliar uplift labor politics

groups.label: Cephalopod Syndicate maintenance crews
institutional_logic: Convert intimate knowledge of zero-g service arteries into mutual aid, extraction power, and industrial disruption when formal systems treat uplift labor as configurable equipment.
material_incentives: Keep maintenance knowledge from becoming management property
Use chokepoints without killing the people living downstream
prestige_markers: A bypass only crew could make
A lockout that saves workers while humiliating management
taboo_triggers: Human-default infrastructure that ignores nonhuman bodies
Managers treating maintenance crews as tools until something breaks
emotional_display_norms: Humor, obliqueness, and technical elegance carry anger without making it easy to prosecute.
trust_defaults: Distrusts management maps and speeches from people who cannot fit through service lungs

cultural_pressure_fields.summary: Recognition is proven through the actual work workers do, the risks they carry, and the refusals they coordinate.
cultural_pressure_fields.projection_use: ALF speakers should argue from shift reality and worker competence, not abstract pity.

cultural_pressure_fields.summary: AU treats construction continuity as authority; a stopped yard is a political wound disguised as missed schedule.
cultural_pressure_fields.projection_use: AU-coded speakers should compress moral conflict into safety, milestones, exposure, and incident containment.

cultural_pressure_fields.summary: Autonomous worker behavior threatens the claim that uplift scaffolding and biodrone limiters are managed design rather than domination.
cultural_pressure_fields.projection_use: BioElevate speakers should reframe personhood evidence as drift, misconfiguration, or misuse.

cultural_pressure_fields.summary: Baseline workers can read uplift recognition as another way the future improves everyone except them.
cultural_pressure_fields.projection_use: Baseline speakers may begin from resentment but can pivot if shared injury and management extraction become visible.

cultural_pressure_fields.summary: Technical leverage is admirable only if it keeps people alive downstream.
cultural_pressure_fields.projection_use: Cephalopod speakers should offer precise, bodily grounded options and resist grand speeches about risk they will personally carry.

observer_read: Organizers are turning manageable equipment behavior into political theater.
target_self_read: Workers are already political because the yard makes their lives disposable.
misread_patterns: AU mistakes coordinated refusal for contamination rather than evidence of shared memory.
factional_misread_matrix.projection_use: Use when management tries to keep the conflict inside safety procedure while workers force recognition language into the room.

observer_read: Recognition for engineered labor may mean baseline crews lose shifts, pay, or dignity first.
target_self_read: The same system disposes of baseline and engineered workers through different categories.
misread_patterns: Baseline fear can confuse solidarity with replacement.
factional_misread_matrix.projection_use: Use when the protagonist must speak to grievance without excusing species panic.

observer_read: Cephalopod maintenance autonomy is useful adaptation until it withholds access.
target_self_read: The yard only notices nonhuman expertise when it needs someone small and soft enough to enter the danger.
misread_patterns: Design staff treat body affordance as product feature; crews treat it as lived labor.
factional_misread_matrix.projection_use: Use when service-lung control becomes political leverage.

role_pressure_fields.summary: A worker-organizer inside the yard who knows enough procedure to keep people alive and enough politics to know procedure will not save them alone.
obligations: Keep the strike from becoming empty spectacle
Translate worker memory into undeniable evidence
failure_modes: Getting a worker hurt for proof

role_pressure_fields.summary: A human maintenance worker whose class grievance can become solidarity or reactionary strikebreaking.
A zero-g service-lung expert whose body and skill make technical leverage possible.
obligations: Keep life support from becoming hostage-taking
Use bypass knowledge without killing downstream civilians
failure_modes: Being reduced to tool or mascot
Withholding too much information from allies

role_pressure_fields.summary: A yard authority trying to preserve construction continuity and liability posture while the workforce demonstrates politics in the machinery.
failure_modes: Treating workers as interchangeable until the yard proves otherwise
Escalating security into a public injury

known_context.text: Knows the yard routines, workers, and ALF organizing cells, but not exactly how far Nara's shared memory extends until the scene evidence appears.

beliefs.text: Believes recognition must emerge from workers' actual competence and risk, not from pity.

misreads.text: May underestimate how much baseline workers hear recognition as replacement.

must_not_know.text: Does not know the final historical outcome of the strike from inside the scene.

known_context.text: Knows baseline crews have been losing status and schedule control to engineered labor systems and augmentation markets.

beliefs.text: Believes AU will sacrifice baseline workers too, but fears the strike will make his crew pay first.

misreads.text: May read engineered worker coordination as management replacement strategy rather than shared labor politics.

must_not_know.text: Does not know Nara's private memories unless Nara demonstrates or shares them in scene.

known_context.text: Knows AU schedule, safety board exposure, and stoppage risk; does not know every service-lung dependency the cephalopod crew can hold.

beliefs.text: Believes keeping the yard operational is the only way to keep everyone from getting hurt and everyone paid.

misreads.text: May read coordinated worker refusal as sabotage rather than proof of political agency.

must_not_know.text: Does not know the exact ALF cell structure unless exposed by branch action.

author_only_boundaries.text: This fixture should not solve the whole Pallas Species Strikes; it should show one ordinary yard beat becoming politically irreversible.
Nara's memory and coordination are evidence inside this fixture, not a license for every character to know engineered workers are generally sentient.

open_questions.question: What exact AU yard map and local union institutions exist at Pallas?
open_questions.notes: The fixture can proceed with provisional cavity-yard details because existing lore grounds AU habitats and strike dynamics enough for a first scaffold.

open_questions.question: Which item blueprint classes should formalize limiter interfaces and zero-g harnesses in Aetheria-Economy?
open_questions.notes: Coordinator artifact records item_manifest_deltas for future economy integration.
